title: login page is broken
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

login page is broken. After I click on I have code, it didn’t scroll & login icon became bad. Also I don’t see start via family code?

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
The login/onboarding page has multiple UI bugs reported by the user:

**What the user reported:**
1. After clicking "I have code" (the existing-user / family-code entry flow), the page did not scroll to the code-entry section.
2. The login icon displayed incorrectly ("became bad") after clicking that button.
3. The "Start via family code" option is not visible at all on the page.

**Component/area affected:**
- Login / onboarding screen (likely src/components/Login.tsx or similar onboarding component)
- The flow for joining an existing family using a shared/family code

**Expected behaviour:**
- Clicking "I have code" should smoothly scroll to a code-entry input field
- The login icon should remain visually correct after the click
- A "Start via family code" option should be clearly visible on the initial screen

**Current behaviour:**
- No scroll occurs after clicking "I have code"
- Login icon renders incorrectly after click
- "Start via family code" option is missing or hidden

**Edge cases to handle:**
- Ensure the scroll works on both mobile and desktop viewport sizes
- Verify icon state is reset properly if the user clicks away and back
- Check if "start via family code" was removed or conditionally hidden and needs to be restored/shown

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Clicking "I have code" scrolls smoothly to the family-code input section
- [ ] The login icon displays correctly before, during, and after clicking "I have code"
- [ ] "Start via family code" option is visible on the login/onboarding screen
- [ ] All three items work correctly on mobile viewport

**Testing:**
- Add a unit/component test that verifies the "I have code" click handler triggers scroll and does not break icon state
- Add a regression test for the presence and visibility of the "start via family code" UI element
- Verify existing login flow tests still pass after the fix

title: wake up is still not syncing other device shows sleeping
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

wake up is still not syncing other device shows sleeping

---

**Category:** `bug` `sleep`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._

implementation_notes.approach: Fixed HomeTab.tsx lastSleepEntry detection to use reduce() scanning for max id instead of .find() which stopped at the first (oldest) match in the ascending-sorted array from mergeEntries. This ensures the most recent sleep entry wins regardless of array order. Also updated polling interval comment from 30s to 60s to match actual behavior. Added 12 unit tests covering empty logs, single entry types, cross-device sync scenarios, and the specific regression case of [Nap(T1), WakeUp(T2)] ascending order.

enhanced_description:
Bug: Wake Up sync not updating sleep state on partner device.

**What the user reported:**
When one device logs a "Wake Up" event, the other paired device continues to show the baby as "sleeping" even after the Wake Up syncs via Firestore.

**Root cause (identified during triage):**
In HomeTab.tsx (~line 163), lastSleepEntry is found using:
  (logs.sleep || []).find(e => e.type === 'Nap' || e.type === 'Night Sleep' || e.type === 'Wake Up')

The syncService.mergeEntries() returns entries sorted ascending by id (oldest first). So .find() returns the OLDEST sleep entry — the "Nap" or "Night Sleep" — even if a newer "Wake Up" has been synced. The wake-up event is in the array but is never seen because .find() stops at the first match.

**Component/area affected:**
- src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx — lastSleepEntry detection (use most recent, not oldest)
- src/utils/syncService.ts — mergeEntries sort order (ascending = oldest first), which compounds the HomeTab bug
- Optionally: src/hooks/useFirebaseSync.ts — ensure requestSync is called after any sleep quick-log action

**Expected behaviour:**
After device A logs a Wake Up, device B syncs (within the polling interval) and correctly shows the baby as awake. The isSleeping flag reflects the MOST RECENT sleep-category entry, not the oldest.

**Current behaviour:**
Device B shows "sleeping" indefinitely because lastSleepEntry resolves to the old Nap/Night Sleep entry (first in ascending-sorted array), ignoring the synced Wake Up entry.

**Fix approach:**
- Change lastSleepEntry to use the LAST matching element instead of the first, e.g. use [...].reverse().find(...) or [...].findLast(...) or pick the entry with the maximum id. The variable name "lastSleepEntry" already implies it should be the most recent.
- Verify requestSync() is invoked after quick-logging a Wake Up on the source device, so the push happens promptly.

**Edge cases to handle:**
- If logs.sleep is empty, lastSleepEntry is undefined — isSleeping should be false (no change needed here, already works).
- The 14-hour auto-expire logic should still apply to the most recent entry.
- Multiple sleep sessions in one day — only the very last entry matters.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] After Device A logs Wake Up, Device B shows awake status within the next poll cycle (~30-60s).
- [ ] isSleeping is computed from the most recent sleep entry, not the oldest.
- [ ] All existing sleep-detection unit tests still pass.
- [ ] The 14-hour auto-expire still functions correctly with the fix.

**Testing:**
- Unit test: logs.sleep with [Nap at T1, Wake Up at T2] → isSleeping should be false.
- Unit test: logs.sleep with only [Nap at T1] → isSleeping should be true.
- Unit test: mergeEntries with remote Wake Up entry arriving after local Nap → merged array has both, most-recent is Wake Up.
- Regression test: Wake Up logged on device A, then mergeEntries run on device B — verify resulting isSleeping computation is false.

title: the actual items - diaper, sleep etc is not encrypted
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

the actual items - diaper, sleep etc is not encrypted.

---

**Category:** `bug` `diaper` `sleep`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._

implementation_notes.approach: Removed all dead unencrypted CRUD helpers from firestoreUtils.ts (saveEntries, getEntries, and all category-specific wrappers: saveFeedEntries, saveDiaperEntries, saveSleepEntries, saveMilestoneEntries, saveMedicineEntries, saveGrowthEntries, savePumpEntries, saveSolidFoodEntries, saveTemperatureEntries, getFeedEntries, getDiaperEntries, getSleepEntries, getMilestoneEntries, getMedicineEntries, getGrowthEntries, getPumpEntries, getSolidFoodEntries, getTemperatureEntries). The only remaining Firestore write/read paths are saveEntriesEncrypted and getEntriesEncrypted. Updated tests to assert that unencrypted helpers no longer exist as exports, verify pushAll uses only saveEntriesEncrypted, and confirm no plaintext data is written to Firestore.

enhanced_description:
User reports that baby log items (diaper, sleep, etc.) appear unencrypted in Firebase.

**What the user reported:**
Log entries for diaper changes, sleep sessions, and other categories can be read in plaintext — they are not encrypted.

**Investigation findings:**
- The CURRENT active sync path (syncService.ts → saveEntriesEncrypted) IS encrypted using AES-256-GCM via the Web Crypto API. The family code is used as the symmetric key via PBKDF2 key derivation.
- However, firestoreUtils.ts still exports dead/unused unencrypted helpers: saveEntries(), getEntries(), and 10+ category-specific wrappers (saveDiaperEntries, getSleepEntries, etc.). These functions are defined but never imported or called from any active code path.
- Old unencrypted Firestore documents may exist from before the encrypted sync was implemented (individual entry docs at families/{code}/profiles/{pid}/{category}/{entryId} vs. the new single encrypted doc at _encrypted).
- The category names themselves (diaper, sleep) are visible in Firestore paths even though document content is encrypted.

**Component/area affected:**
- src/utils/firestoreUtils.ts — dead unencrypted helpers (saveEntries, getEntries, saveFeedEntries, saveDiaperEntries, etc.)
- src/utils/syncService.ts — verify pushAll/pullAndMerge use only encrypted helpers (currently OK)

**Expected behaviour:**
No plaintext log data is ever written to Firestore. Only encrypted { ct, iv, ts } documents exist under each category.

**Current behaviour:**
Dead unencrypted code paths exist in firestoreUtils.ts (risk of accidental use in future). Old unencrypted Firestore documents may still be present from a prior sync.

**Fix approach:**
1. Remove the dead unencrypted CRUD helpers from firestoreUtils.ts (saveEntries, getEntries, and all unencrypted category-specific exports). The only Firestore write path should be saveEntriesEncrypted/getEntriesEncrypted.
2. Verify via grep/audit that no other file imports or calls these removed helpers.
3. Optionally: on first sync after this change, scan for and delete any old-format plaintext documents (individual entry docs that are NOT named _encrypted) under each category.

**Edge cases to handle:**
- If old plaintext docs exist in Firestore, the pull step should skip/ignore them (getEntriesEncrypted already does this — it only reads the _encrypted doc).
- Key derivation is cached, so removing unused code should not affect performance.
- The family code is visible in the Firestore path — this is acceptable (it is the shared secret, not sensitive on its own).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] firestoreUtils.ts no longer exports any unencrypted saveEntries / getEntries helpers
- [ ] All Firestore writes in the active codebase use saveEntriesEncrypted
- [ ] No plaintext log data fields appear in newly written Firestore documents
- [ ] Existing sync tests pass after the cleanup

**Testing:**
- Add a unit test asserting that saveEntries and saveFeedEntries (unencrypted) no longer exist as exports from firestoreUtils.ts.
- Add a test for pushAll verifying it calls saveEntriesEncrypted (not saveEntries) for each category.
- Verify existing sync unit tests still pass after dead code removal.

title: all data in firebase should be encrypted
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

all data in firebase should be encrypted

---

**Category:** `enhancement`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
skip_reason: Already implemented by #167. All 11 log categories (feed, diaper, sleep, tummy, allergy, bath, massage, growth, temp, meds, pump) are encrypted with AES-256-GCM via saveEntriesEncrypted. Profile metadata, family code, and settings are stored only in IndexedDB — never written to Firestore. #167 removed the last dead unencrypted Firestore helpers, leaving only the encrypted path active. The only unencrypted Firestore fields are the IV (cryptographically required for AES-GCM), timestamps (no sensitive content), and path components (category names, family code in path — by design).

title: nurse left and nurse right are not maintaining order and are at different pla...
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

nurse left and nurse right are not maintaining order and are at different places even though it was implemented earlier

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
implementation_notes: Fixed Nurse Left / Nurse Right stable ordering in quick-log grid. Two root causes addressed: (1) usage-count sort could place Nurse Right before Nurse Left when Right was tapped more — fixed by adding explicit guard at top of sort comparator; (2) priority/warning promotions in topItems could reorder the pair after pinning — fixed by adding a post-topItems swap to enforce Left-before-Right. Files changed: src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx. Tests added: tests/unit/nurseButtonOrder.test.ts (covers usage inversion, active timer, and age-transition scenarios).
enhanced_description:
The "Nurse Left" and "Nurse Right" quick-log buttons in the HomeTab all-actions grid are not maintaining a stable, consistent order — they appear at inconsistent positions even though an ordering/pinning fix was previously implemented.

**What the user reported:**
- "Nurse Left" and "Nurse Right" buttons are out of order relative to each other (e.g., "Nurse Right" appearing before "Nurse Left")
- Their grid position changes between sessions or interactions, despite a prior implementation intended to stabilize their placement

**Component/area affected:**
- src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx — the all-actions quick-log grid, specifically:
  - Age-based priority ordering (Nurse Left = 1, Nurse Right = 2)
  - Usage-count sort logic (kicks in at ≥10 total taps, sorts by most-used first — this can override the Left-before-Right age priority)
  - Pinning logic that should keep Nurse Left and Right together at columns 0-1 in row 0
  - The sortKey property used to keep position stable when buttons switch to "Switch Left"/"Switch Right" labels

**Expected behaviour:**
- "Nurse Left" always appears at a fixed position (e.g., first/leftmost nursing button)
- "Nurse Right" always appears immediately after/beside "Nurse Left"
- Their relative order (Left before Right) never changes regardless of usage counts or active timer state
- When one button transforms to "Switch Left/Right" during an active timer, both buttons stay in the same grid positions

**Current behaviour:**
- Button positions are inconsistent — they can appear at different locations in the grid between sessions or after interactions
- Likely root cause: the usage-count sort (primary sort criterion when total taps ≥ 10) overrides the age-based priority, so if "Nurse Right" is tapped more than "Nurse Left", it sorts first — breaking the expected Left→Right order
- Pinning logic may not correctly enforce Left-before-Right ordering within the pinned row

**Edge cases to handle:**
- When an active breast timer is running (one side switches to "Switch Left/Right"), positions must remain stable
- Usage counts heavily favouring one side must not reorder the pair — Left always precedes Right
- First-run state (< 10 total taps) vs. usage-count-active state must both yield the same Left→Right order
- Age transitions (under/over 6 months, 12 months) must not affect the relative Left/Right ordering

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] "Nurse Left" always appears before (to the left of) "Nurse Right" in the grid
- [ ] Both buttons remain in the same grid positions regardless of usage count or active timer state
- [ ] The fix is stable across page refreshes and multiple logging sessions
- [ ] When buttons display as "Switch Left"/"Switch Right", their grid positions are unchanged from the normal "Nurse Left"/"Nurse Right" positions

**Testing:**
- Unit test: when nurseLeft taps < nurseRight taps (usage-count inversion scenario), sortedActions must still place Nurse Left before Nurse Right
- Unit test: with an active Breast R timer (left button becomes "Switch Left"), verify both buttons remain at same grid indices as without active timer
- Unit test: age = 3 months (< 6mo priority), age = 8 months (6-12mo priority), age = 14 months (12+mo, lower base priority) — all must preserve Left-before-Right order
- Regression: existing nurseSwitchBtn.test.ts tests must still pass after the fix
